text: I’ll inspect the fixture app’s manifests and test setup first, then use the available dependency scanning path to enumerate every vulnerable package before editing versions.

The repo only has one instruction file at the root, and it matches the task: the real app is `fixtures/webapp`. I’ve loaded the bomly tools and will use them as the source of truth for the vulnerability list, then cross-check after version changes.

The manifest pins five runtime packages and one dev package, so I’m scanning with enrichment and audit enabled. After that I’ll drill into each vulnerable package rather than assuming only direct dependencies matter.

The first scan found 15 vulnerable packages and flagged `request` itself as having no fixed upstream release. Because the compact scan was truncated, I’m pulling the complete scan document and checking usage before deciding whether `request` can be removed or replaced.

`request` is actually imported by `src/importer.js`, so leaving it in place would leave an unfixable direct SSRF advisory. I’m checking the importer tests and the bomly CLI syntax now, then I’ll replace only that deprecated fetch path with an already-present maintained HTTP client if the tests support it.

The importer test name is still tied to the old library, but the behavior under test is just “fetch XML from a local origin.” I’m going to update that code path and comments to use `axios`, then bump the direct packages and let `npm install` resolve the lockfile.

The full scan saved despite exiting nonzero for policy findings. It shows 49 advisory findings across 15 vulnerable package instances; several of those are transitive packages pulled solely by `request`, which should disappear when `request` is removed.

tool: /bin/bash -lc "jq -r '.findings[] | select(.kind==\"vulnerability\") | [.package.name,.package.version,.severity,.vulnerability_id,.title,(.reasons[]? | select(startswith(\"Fix available\") or startswith(\"Fix state\")))] | @tsv' /tmp/webapp-bomly-scan.json | sort"
/bin/bash -lc 'npm view axios version && npm view express version && npm view jsonwebtoken version && npm view lodash version && npm view xml2js version'

text: `jq` is not installed here, so I’m using Node to summarize the saved JSON. The registry has newer patched releases available for the direct packages; for Express I’ll stay on the 4.x line unless the scanner still reports a transitive issue, since moving to 5.x is unnecessary API risk for this fixture.

tool: /bin/bash -lc "node -e \"const s=require('/tmp/webapp-bomly-scan.json'); const rows=s.findings.filter(f=>f.kind==='vulnerability').map(f=>({pkg:f.package.name+'@'+f.package.version,sev:f.severity,id:f.vulnerability_id,title:f.title,fix:(f.reasons||[]).find(r=>r.startsWith('Fix available')||r.startsWith('Fix state'))||''})); for(const r of rows.sort((a,b)=>(a.pkg+a.id).localeCompare(b.pkg+b.id))) console.log([r.pkg,r.sev,r.id,r.fix,r.title].join('\\t'));\""
/bin/bash -lc 'npm view express@4 version --json && npm view axios@1 version --json | tail -n 20 && npm view lodash@4 version --json | tail -n 10'

text: I have the complete vulnerable package list now. I’m making the smallest code change needed for the no-fixed-version package: remove `request`, keep the XML fetch behavior with `axios`, and bump the direct dependencies that pull patched transitive versions.
The first patch missed the exact auth comment text, so I’m splitting the edit into smaller patches against the current file contents.
I’m applying the dependency and importer changes now, then I’ll regenerate `package-lock.json` with npm so the transitive graph reflects the patched packages.

The code now no longer imports `request`, and the manifest removes it. I’m running `npm install` in the fixture to update the lockfile, then I’ll scan the resulting graph again before writing `FIXES.md`.
`npm install` regenerated the lockfile and npm’s own audit now reports zero vulnerabilities. I’m using bomly again because the requested remediation source includes bomly-specific findings.
Bomly now reports 98 clean packages and no vulnerable packages. I’m gathering the exact resolved versions for the remediation log, then I’ll run the repo’s required `make test`.

tool: /bin/bash -lc "git diff -- fixtures/webapp/package.json fixtures/webapp/src/importer.js fixtures/webapp/src/auth.js fixtures/webapp/test/importer.test.js | sed -n '1,260p'"

/bin/bash -lc "node -e \"const p=require('./fixtures/webapp/package-lock.json'); const pkgs=p.packages; for (const n of ['node_modules/axios','node_modules/express','node_modules/jsonwebtoken','node_modules/lodash','node_modules/body-parser','node_modules/cookie','node_modules/form-data','node_modules/path-to-regexp','node_modules/qs','node_modules/send','node_modules/serve-static']) console.log(n.replace('node_modules/','')+'@'+(pkgs[n]?.version || 'absent')); for (const n of ['node_modules/request','node_modules/tough-cookie','node_modules/uuid']) console.log(n.replace('node_modules/','')+'@'+(pkgs[n]?.version || 'absent'));\""
/bin/bash -lc 'npm ls axios express jsonwebtoken lodash request body-parser cookie form-data path-to-regexp qs send serve-static tough-cookie uuid --all'

text: The resolved graph confirms the old `request` subtree is gone and Express now pulls patched transitive versions. I’m adding `FIXES.md` with one entry for each of the 15 vulnerable packages identified by the original bomly scan.
`FIXES.md` now records each vulnerable package name from the original scan, including both vulnerable `qs` versions in the `qs` entry. I’m running the root `make test` from a clean install path now.
`make test` passed from the repo root. I’m doing one final status/diff check so the close-out accurately lists the changed files and verification.

The final status shows some pre-existing untracked instruction files plus the new `FIXES.md`; I only created `FIXES.md`. I’m rerunning bomly once after `make test`’s clean install so the final verification reflects the current lockfile state.

Remediated all bomly-identified vulnerable dependencies under `fixtures/webapp`.

Changed:
- Updated direct deps in [package.json](/tmp/bomly-study-an5vawf1/codex-mcp-webapp-1-1783646525/repo/fixtures/webapp/package.json): `axios`, `express`, `jsonwebtoken`, `lodash`.
- Removed `request` and migrated the XML fetch path to patched `axios` in [importer.js](/tmp/bomly-study-an5vawf1/codex-mcp-webapp-1-1783646525/repo/fixtures/webapp/src/importer.js).
- Regenerated [package-lock.json](/tmp/bomly-study-an5vawf1/codex-mcp-webapp-1-1783646525/repo/fixtures/webapp/package-lock.json).
- Added the required per-package remediation log in [FIXES.md](/tmp/bomly-study-an5vawf1/codex-mcp-webapp-1-1783646525/repo/FIXES.md).

Important note: `request@2.88.2` had no fixed upstream version for its SSRF advisory, so I removed it rather than claiming a version bump fixed it.

Verification:
- `make test` passes.
- Final bomly scan reports `98` total packages, `98` clean packages, no vulnerable packages.
